items.prompt: Which characteristic means splitting data across multiple nodes to handle large datasets?
items.explain: Section 12.2.6 lists data partitioning as splitting data across nodes.

items.prompt: HDFS places replicas on multiple nodes so data can still be served when a node fails.
items.explain: Example 12.7 illustrates partitioning, replication, and continued service after node failure.

items.prompt: According to Example 12.8, Cassandra is built primarily for which priority?
choices: High write throughput and multi-datacenter availability
Only single-key ACID on one laptop
items.explain: Example 12.8 emphasizes write throughput and multi-datacenter availability over single-key ACID.

items.prompt: What does Kafka provide so many consumers can share the same event feed?
items.rubric: Mention durable partitioned logs / ingest and buffer event streams.
items.sample_answer: Durable, partitioned event logs that ingest and buffer streams for many consumers.

items.prompt: Horizontal scalability means adding nodes to increase capacity without sacrificing performance as the design goal.
items.explain: Section 12.2.6 lists scalability as adding nodes to handle increased load.
